notes: Lamar Jackson restructured his contract on March 12, converting $51.25M of salary into a bonus and clearing ~$40M in 2026 cap space (reducing his cap hit from ~$74.5M to ~$34.5M). OverTheCap shows $44.9M in current team cap space post-FA signings. The failed Maxx Crosby trade (Ravens backed out due to medical concerns) preserved two first-round picks (2026 and 2027) that would have been sent to Las Vegas.

key_departures.contract_signed: 3yr/$81M ($27M AAV) — record deal for interior OL
key_departures.impact: Three-time Pro Bowl center and one of the top-5 centers in the NFL. Departure leaves a massive hole at C. In-house option Corey Bullock (ERFA, retained) is a depth piece, not a starter-caliber replacement. No veteran free-agent center was signed to fill the void.

Versatile receiving TE and valued backup to Mark Andrews. Loss removes the only true pass-catching weapon behind Andrews. Durham Smythe (blocking TE) was added but is not a receiving replacement for Likely.

key_departures.contract_signed: 3yr/$24.3M — highest-paid blocking TE in NFL history
key_departures.impact: Primary in-line blocking TE in Baltimore's run-heavy offense. Durham Smythe was signed as a direct replacement, partially addressing this departure.
Productive rotational edge rusher (2.5 sacks, 15 QB hits in 9 games in 2025). Departure reduces EDGE depth behind Hendrickson and Mike Green. Partly offset by Hendrickson signing.
Former 2nd-round pick who never fulfilled draft promise due to injuries (4.5 career sacks). Leaves Ravens with thin EDGE depth. Mike Green and Tavius Robinson remain.

Starting safety who helped stabilize the Ravens' secondary. Combined with Ar'Darius Washington's departure, left a total void at the S3 role behind Kyle Hamilton and Malaki Starks. Addressed by Hawkins signing.
Rotational safety who tore his Achilles in OTAs and played only 4 games in 2025. Followed former HC John Harbaugh to New York. Minimal on-field impact but part of double safety departure.
First-team All-Pro punter in his contract year (44.9 net YPP). Departure forces Ravens to find a replacement punter. Special teams need created.
Special teams contributor with 18 tackles in 2025. Depth loss only.
RFA not tendered. Derrick Henry, Justice Hill, and Rasheen Ali remain at RB. Minimal roster impact.
Veteran with only 2 sacks in 2025 after 12.5-sack Pro Bowl season two years prior. Clear decline. Depth loss only.

signings.analysis: The biggest outside free agent signing in Ravens franchise history. Four-time Pro Bowler and 2024 NFL sacks leader (17.5). One of only five players in NFL history with 17+ sacks in back-to-back seasons (2023–24). The $60M guaranteed on a 4-year deal is a clear long-term commitment. EDGE goes from tier-1 critical to tier-3 depth/developmental need — Ravens may want a rotational EDGE in mid rounds but the starter problem is solved. Signed just hours after the Maxx Crosby trade fell through on medical concerns, preserving Baltimore's 2026 and 2027 first-round picks in the process. Knock is run defense (16% run stop win rate over last 5 seasons, last among EDGE) and an injury (core muscle surgery) that limited him to 7 games in 2025.

A reunion for Simpson, who started every game for Baltimore in 2023 before 2 seasons with the Jets. $17.5M fully guaranteed at signing including a $10.5M signing bonus — a real investment at guard. He projects as the starting right guard, with 2025 3rd-round pick Emery Jones Jr. likely competing at LG. Addresses the GUARD need but does NOT solve center — Linderbaum's departure still leaves a massive hole at C with only ERFA Corey Bullock as an in-house option. IOL partially addressed (one guard starter added, center remains critical).

15-game starter for the AFC Champion Patriots in 2025, leading the team with 4 interceptions. PFF coverage grade of 74.7 (12th among safeties) and 79.7 run defense grade (15th). Fills the S3 role behind Kyle Hamilton and Malaki Starks, allowing Hamilton continued freedom to roam as a quasi-linebacker. However, the 2-year deal at $5M AAV signals a bridge/depth signing rather than a long-term solution. Safety depth remains thin and the Ravens may still want a developmental safety in the draft.

Direct replacement for blocking TE Charlie Kolar. Reunites with new OC Declan Doyle, who coached him in Chicago. Blocked on 74.1% of offensive snaps for the Bears, who ranked 7th in rushing. However, he was PFF's lowest-graded TE in 2025 overall and is a minimal pass-catcher (4 catches, 25 yards in 2025). The 1-year deal is depth/prove-it money. Kolar's blocking departure is minimally addressed; Likely's pass-catching departure is NOT addressed — TE remains a genuine need, particularly at the receiving TE role.
signings.notes: Contract terms reported as a 1-year deal; exact guaranteed amount not fully confirmed but estimated at near full value given short term. ESPN/NFL reporters confirmed the agreement.

signings.analysis: Re-signing Huntley as the backup QB behind Lamar Jackson. Huntley won both starts in 2025, completing 77.6% of passes with a 103.1 rating and 151 rushing yards. Cooper Rush was cut as a result. QB depth (backup) was a soft need and it is now resolved. The Ravens committed 2 years, confirming they view Huntley as a legitimate safety net for Jackson — critical given Jackson has missed 4+ games in 3 of the last 5 seasons.

Re-signing Awuzie, who was arguably the Ravens' best CB in 2025 despite only 5 starts (13th-highest PFF CB grade among players with 20%+ snap share). 7 PBUs and 55.6% completion rate allowed — excellent cover man. The $5M fully guaranteed 1-year deal is a bargain but signals short-term thinking. He profiles as CB3 behind Marlon Humphrey (starter) and Nate Wiggins (starter), though both had rough 2025 seasons. One-year deal = bridge. CB still needs long-term answers, particularly with Humphrey turning 30 and Wiggins still developing. Need is minimally addressed — CB remains a viable mid-round draft target.

International Pathway Player retained via ERFA tender. Had 15 tackles, 1 sack in 13 games (214 defensive snaps) in 2025. Developmental rotational piece. Minimum deal — does not move the needle on IDL need at all. IDL remains a genuine need alongside Nnamdi Madubuike (recovering from neck injury, availability uncertain).

ERFA tendered at minimum. Bullock was Linderbaum's backup and the most likely internal candidate to start at center in 2026 by default. However, he is an undrafted player with minimal starting experience — a big step up. His retention does not resolve the center need; it merely provides a warm body. Center remains a critical draft/free-agency target.

One-year deal for depth OL who has played only 9 offensive snaps in 3 NFL seasons. Reunites with OL coach Dwayne Ledford (coached Gwyn at Atlanta). Camp/depth body. Does not address the IOL starting need whatsoever.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Hendrickson signing (4yr/$112M, $60M guaranteed) fully addresses the starter need. Ravens may still want developmental depth/rotational EDGE (Green + Robinson are young and unproven), but this is no longer a premium draft need. Day 2 or early Day 3 pick at most.

Linderbaum's departure to Las Vegas (3yr/$81M) escalates this from a guard-focused need to a critical center + guard need. Simpson (3yr/$30M) addresses one guard spot. But center has NO viable starter on roster — Corey Bullock is an ERFA undrafted player. IOL is now the single most critical remaining need entering the draft. Center specifically should be a top-60 pick priority.

Losing both Likely (pass-catcher, 3yr/$40M to Giants) and Kolar (blocker, 3yr/$24.3M to Chargers) was a significant double-departure. Smythe (1yr/$2.5M) replaces Kolar in the blocking role only — a minimal substitution. The receiving TE vacancy behind Mark Andrews (35 in 2026) is unaddressed. TE remains a tier-2 need, especially with Andrews aging. Rounds 3-5 target.

Both Gilman and Washington departed, but Hawkins (2yr/$10M) was a legitimate starter for the AFC Champion Patriots in 2025 (4 INTs, 74.7 PFF coverage grade). He fills the S3 role behind Hamilton and Starks competently. Safety drops from a clear need to a mid-round depth target — the Ravens might still want a developmental safety in rounds 4-6.

Awuzie re-signed (1yr/$5M fully guaranteed) but the deal structure signals one year of bridge depth. He's CB3. Humphrey (30, regressed badly in 2025) and Wiggins (entering year 3 with development questions) are the starters. No long-term CB2 solution has been added. CB remains a genuine need — ideally addressed in rounds 2-3 with a young corner who can develop.

Dre'Mont Jones (starter-level production in 2025) and Brent Urban (depth) departed. Nnamdi Madubuike's health remains uncertain after a 2025 neck injury — he's under contract but availability is unclear. Okoye (minimum) retained but is developmental. IDL remains a critical need. Travis Jones is on the roster but not an every-down starter. Should target IDL in rounds 2-4.

No significant additions or losses at WR in free agency. Zay Flowers (approaching contract year) and Rashod Bateman (trade rumors) create uncertainty. DeAndre Hopkins (330 yards in 2025) remains unsigned. WR2/3 remains an unaddressed need. Should target WR in rounds 2-4.

Jordan Stout (First-Team All-Pro, 44.9 net YPP) signed a record-breaking punter deal with the Giants. The Ravens have no replacement punter. Special teams need created from scratch. Should address via free agency or mid-to-late draft.

key_additions.headline: 2024 NFL sacks leader (17.5) and four-time Pro Bowler fills Baltimore's biggest need on a franchise-record free-agent deal
Returning guard stabilizes one IOL spot but center vacancy created by Linderbaum's departure remains unsolved
AFC Champion Patriots starter (4 INTs, 15 starts in 2025) fills the S3 role vacated by Gilman and Washington
Blocking TE replacement for Charlie Kolar reunites with OC Declan Doyle but does not address the receiving TE vacancy
Dual-threat backup who won both 2025 starts retained; Cooper Rush released
Team's best CB in 2025 (13th PFF grade) brought back on a bargain 1-year bridge deal

key_losses.contract_summary: 3yr/$81M with Las Vegas Raiders ($27M AAV — record for interior OL)
key_losses.headline: Three-time Pro Bowl center departs on a record-breaking deal, leaving an enormous hole at C that was not replaced in free agency
Versatile receiving TE follows former HC John Harbaugh to New York, leaving Andrews without a pass-catching complement

key_losses.contract_summary: 3yr/$24.3M with LA Chargers — highest-paid blocking TE in NFL history
key_losses.headline: Primary blocking TE and key run-game weapon departs, partially replaced by Durham Smythe
Productive rotational edge rusher (2.5 sacks, 15 QB hits in 9 games) departs, reducing depth behind new star Hendrickson
Starting safety signs big deal with Chiefs; Hawkins signing partially replaces his production

key_losses.contract_summary: 3yr/$12.3M with New York Giants — record punter deal
key_losses.headline: First-Team All-Pro punter follows Harbaugh to New York on a record contract, creating an unaddressed special teams need
Former 2nd-round pick who never fulfilled his draft potential departs for fresh start with division rival

confidence_notes: All major contract figures sourced from OverTheCap and Spotrac. Hendrickson ($112M/$60M gtd), Simpson ($30M/$17.5M gtd), and Hawkins ($10M) are fully confirmed and filed. Smythe contract terms (1yr) confirmed by multiple reporters; exact guarantee amount estimated near full value. Awuzie (1yr/$5M fully guaranteed) confirmed by Garafolo/Zrebiec. Huntley (2yr/$5M) confirmed by ESPN. Ojabo and Dre'Mont Jones departure destinations confirmed; Jones contract figure reported as $36.5M (Yahoo/CBS) and $39M (SI) — range noted. Cap space figure from OverTheCap as of 3/16/2026.
